source: GICS sector SPDRs (State Street), major index ETFs, and hand-built thematic constituent baskets (Mag 7, semis, memory, software, AI-infra, banks, homebuilders, nuclear/power). 'sectorMap' adds, per GICS sector, its top constituent companies (for the company-by-sector momentum heatmap) and a shortlist of the most prominent sector/segment ETFs with reference size/cost/coverage params (for the sector-ETF selector). Constituents, ETF lists and all reference numbers (AUM $M, expense ratio, holdings count) are APPROXIMATE, US-listed only, captured ~2026-07 — verify current membership/figures with the issuer/index. All analytics are computed from LIVE fetched daily price history, not from anything stored here.
note: Three entry TYPES in entries[]. (1) type:'sector' or type:'index' → a single-ticker ETF proxy (only 'ticker' is required). (2) type:'group' → a synthetic EQUAL-WEIGHT, daily-rebalanced basket built from 'members' (US-listed tickers); this also unlocks the breadth panel (% of members above their 50/200-day average, advancers/decliners). Add or remove an entry by editing entries[]. 'on':true default-includes it. 'sectorMap' is a SEPARATE reference catalog keyed by the 11 GICS sector ids: sectorMap[XLx].constituents = top companies to render in the company heatmap; sectorMap[XLx].etfs = candidate ETFs (with issuer/aum/expense/holdings/weighting/segment) ranked in the sector-ETF selector. The two new panels fetch their tickers ON DEMAND (their own buttons) so they never slow the core rotation fetch. This file is fetched at load; if unreachable the tool falls back to an inline copy. Fewer default-on entries = a faster first fetch (Twelve Data free tier is throttled).

entries.label: Technology
entries.desc: Technology Select Sector SPDR — hardware, semiconductors and software megacaps (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AVGO).

entries.label: Communication Svcs
entries.desc: Communication Services Select Sector SPDR — GOOGL, META, NFLX, telecom and media.

entries.label: Consumer Discret.
entries.desc: Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR — AMZN, TSLA, HD, retail, autos, travel.

entries.label: Consumer Staples
entries.desc: Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR — defensives: PG, COST, KO, WMT. Classic late-cycle / risk-off leader.

entries.label: Energy
entries.desc: Energy Select Sector SPDR — XOM, CVX, oil & gas. Commodity/inflation-sensitive rotation vehicle.

entries.label: Financials
entries.desc: Financial Select Sector SPDR — banks, insurers, payments (JPM, BRK.B, V, MA). Rate- and curve-sensitive.

entries.label: Health Care
entries.desc: Health Care Select Sector SPDR — LLY, UNH, JNJ, pharma/biotech/devices. Defensive tilt.

entries.label: Industrials
entries.desc: Industrial Select Sector SPDR — GE, CAT, aerospace, machinery, rails. Early-cycle / reflation proxy.

entries.label: Materials
entries.desc: Materials Select Sector SPDR — LIN, chemicals, metals, mining. Commodity-cycle sensitive.

entries.label: Real Estate
entries.desc: Real Estate Select Sector SPDR — REITs. Highly rate-sensitive.

entries.label: Utilities
entries.desc: Utility Select Sector SPDR — defensives + the AI-power-demand theme (CEG, VST, NEE). Bond-proxy.

entries.label: Nasdaq 100
entries.desc: Invesco QQQ — Nasdaq-100 large-cap growth/tech. Growth-vs-value rotation gauge.

entries.label: Russell 2000
entries.desc: iShares Russell 2000 — small caps. Risk-appetite / breadth barometer vs large caps.

entries.label: S&P 500 Equal Wt
entries.desc: Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight — RSP vs SPY reveals megacap concentration vs broad participation.

entries.label: Dow 30
entries.desc: SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average — price-weighted blue chips; value/cyclical lean.

entries.label: S&P MidCap 400
entries.desc: SPDR S&P MidCap 400 — the mid-cap slice; useful in the size-rotation read.

entries.label: Magnificent 7
entries.desc: The seven megacaps that dominate index returns. Equal-weight basket — breadth panel shows how many of the 7 are still individually in uptrends.

entries.label: Semiconductors
entries.desc: Broad semiconductor complex — logic, equipment and analog. The cyclical heartbeat of the tech tape.

entries.label: Memory & Storage
entries.desc: US-listed DRAM/NAND & storage names (Samsung/SK Hynix are not US-listed). Highly cyclical pricing; leads/lags the broader semis.

entries.label: Software
entries.desc: Application & infrastructure software incl. security and data. Higher-duration growth; rate-sensitive.

entries.label: AI Datacenter/Infra
entries.desc: AI buildout picks-and-shovels — accelerators, networking, servers, power & cooling. Overlaps semis but adds power/electrical.

entries.label: Big Banks
entries.desc: Money-center + investment banks. Yield-curve and credit-cycle sensitive; a cyclical-rotation tell.

entries.label: Homebuilders
entries.desc: Homebuilders — rate-sensitive early-cycle group that often leads at rate-cycle turns.

entries.label: Nuclear & Power
entries.desc: Power generation + nuclear/uranium riding AI datacenter electricity demand. Newer names (SMR, OKLO) have short history — the basket handles that gracefully.

entries.label: Bitcoin
entries.desc: Bitcoin (USD). Provider symbols differ (Yahoo BTC-USD, Twelve Data BTC/USD) — resolved automatically. Trades 7 days/week; correlation uses only overlapping weekdays. Watch its correlation to software/growth and to gold.

entries.label: Ethereum
entries.desc: Ethereum (USD). Same provider-symbol handling as BTC.

entries.label: Gold
entries.desc: SPDR Gold Shares — the classic risk-off / real-rates hedge. Gold-vs-BTC and gold-vs-equities correlation is a core rotation tell.

entries.label: Silver
entries.desc: iShares Silver Trust — higher-beta precious metal; industrial + monetary demand.

entries.label: Long Treasuries
entries.desc: iShares 20+ Year Treasury — long-duration bonds. Its correlation to equities flips sign across regimes (a key macro signal).

entries.label: High-Yield Credit
entries.desc: iShares iBoxx High Yield Corporate — credit risk appetite; leads equity stress.

entries.label: Crude Oil
entries.desc: United States Oil Fund — crude proxy; drives Energy-sector relative strength and inflation expectations.

entries.label: US Dollar
entries.desc: Invesco DB US Dollar Bullish — the dollar. A stronger dollar pressures commodities, gold, EM and large-cap multinationals.

etfs.name: Technology Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Technology sector, cap-weighted and top-heavy in AAPL/MSFT/NVDA. The default sector vehicle; classifies V/MA as financials, GOOGL/META as comm-svcs (so 'tech' here is narrower than the mega-cap growth basket).

etfs.name: Vanguard Information Technology ETF
etfs.desc: Full US IT sector incl. mid/small caps — broader coverage than XLK, cheapest large sector-tech fund.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Technology ETF
etfs.desc: US tech incl. some comm-svcs names (GOOGL/META) — a more mega-cap-growth tilt than XLK. Higher fee.

etfs.name: Fidelity MSCI Information Technology ETF
etfs.desc: MSCI US IMI IT — near-identical coverage to VGT at a rock-bottom fee.

etfs.name: VanEck Semiconductor ETF
etfs.desc: The 25 largest semis (NVDA/TSM/AVGO-heavy) — concentrated, the cyclical high-beta slice of tech, not the whole sector.

etfs.name: iShares Semiconductor ETF
etfs.desc: ~30 US-listed semis — slightly broader/less concentrated than SMH.

etfs.name: iShares Expanded Tech-Software ETF
etfs.desc: Application & systems software (MSFT/ORCL/CRM/PANW) — the higher-duration, rate-sensitive software slice.

etfs.name: Communication Services Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Comm-Svcs sector — GOOGL/META dominate (~40% combined). Concentrated by construction.

etfs.name: Vanguard Communication Services ETF
etfs.desc: Broader comm-svcs incl. more mid/small caps than XLC, at the same low fee.

etfs.name: Fidelity MSCI Communication Services ETF
etfs.desc: MSCI US comm-svcs — near-VOX coverage at the lowest fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Telecommunications ETF
etfs.desc: Narrow telecom (T/VZ/TMUS + carriers) — the defensive, dividend-heavy slice, NOT the GOOGL/META growth engine.

etfs.name: Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Discretionary sector — AMZN + TSLA are ~40% combined, so it is really a mega-cap duo plus retail/autos/travel.

etfs.name: Vanguard Consumer Discretionary ETF
etfs.desc: Broader discretionary incl. mid/small caps; same AMZN/TSLA top but a longer tail.

etfs.name: Fidelity MSCI Consumer Discretionary ETF
etfs.desc: MSCI US discretionary — VCR-like coverage at the lowest fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF
etfs.desc: Homebuilders + suppliers (DHI/LEN/PHM) — the rate-sensitive early-cycle slice of discretionary.

etfs.name: SPDR S&P Homebuilders ETF
etfs.desc: Homebuilders + building products, more equal-weight than ITB (more retailer/supplier exposure).

etfs.name: SPDR S&P Retail ETF
etfs.desc: Equal-weight retail — a broad, small-cap-tilted read on the consumer, very different from AMZN-heavy XLY.

etfs.name: Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Staples sector — classic late-cycle / risk-off defensive; COST/WMT/PG-led.

etfs.name: Vanguard Consumer Staples ETF
etfs.desc: Broader staples incl. mid/small caps at the same low fee.

etfs.name: Fidelity MSCI Consumer Staples ETF
etfs.desc: MSCI US staples — VDC-like at the lowest fee.

etfs.name: iShares Global Consumer Staples ETF
etfs.desc: GLOBAL staples (adds Nestlé/Unilever ADRs) — use only if you want ex-US defensive exposure.

etfs.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Energy sector — XOM+CVX ~40%. The most liquid oil-&-gas rotation vehicle.

etfs.name: Vanguard Energy ETF
etfs.desc: Broader energy incl. mid/small E&P and services at a low fee.

etfs.name: SPDR S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production ETF
etfs.desc: Equal-weight E&P — pure upstream, much higher beta to the oil price than XLE.

etfs.name: VanEck Oil Services ETF
etfs.desc: Oil-services (SLB/HAL) — the highest-beta, most cyclical slice of energy.

etfs.name: Alerian MLP ETF
etfs.desc: Midstream MLPs — income/pipeline exposure, K-1-free but high fee and a different tax/return profile.

etfs.name: Financial Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Financials sector — banks + V/MA payments + insurers. Rate- and curve-sensitive; the default financials vehicle.

etfs.name: Vanguard Financials ETF
etfs.desc: Broader financials incl. mid/small banks, low fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Financials ETF
etfs.desc: US financials incl. some real-estate exposure; higher fee than XLF/VFH.

etfs.name: Invesco KBW Bank ETF
etfs.desc: Large money-center + super-regional banks — the pure rate/curve play, no payments or insurers.

etfs.name: SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF
etfs.desc: Equal-weight regional banks — the highest-beta credit/curve slice; the 2023 stress epicentre.

etfs.name: Health Care Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Health-Care sector — LLY/UNH/JNJ-led; a defensive-tilt rotation vehicle.

etfs.name: Vanguard Health Care ETF
etfs.desc: Broader health care incl. mid/small biotech and devices, low fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Healthcare ETF
etfs.desc: US health care, cap-weighted; higher fee than XLV/VHT.

etfs.name: iShares Biotechnology ETF
etfs.desc: Cap-weighted biotech (large-cap led) — the higher-beta, rate-sensitive slice of health care.

etfs.name: SPDR S&P Biotech ETF
etfs.desc: Equal-weight biotech — small-cap-heavy, the highest-beta risk-appetite gauge in health care.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Medical Devices ETF
etfs.desc: Medical devices (ISRG/ABT/MDT) — a steadier, less binary sub-industry than biotech.

etfs.name: Industrial Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Industrials sector — aerospace, machinery, rails, electricals. Early-cycle / reflation proxy, well-diversified (no single name >6%).

etfs.name: Vanguard Industrials ETF
etfs.desc: Broader industrials incl. mid/small caps and transports, low fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Industrials ETF
etfs.desc: US industrials, cap-weighted; higher fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF
etfs.desc: Aerospace & defense (RTX/BA/LMT) — a defensive-cyclical sub-industry driven by defense budgets + air travel.

etfs.name: Global X U.S. Infrastructure Development ETF
etfs.desc: Domestic infrastructure (materials/electricals/machinery) — a reshoring/capex theme that overlaps industrials + materials.

etfs.name: Materials Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Materials sector — chemicals-heavy (LIN ~17%), plus metals/mining and packaging. Commodity-cycle sensitive.

etfs.name: Vanguard Materials ETF
etfs.desc: Broader materials incl. mid/small caps, low fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Basic Materials ETF
etfs.desc: US basic materials, cap-weighted; higher fee, smaller/less liquid.

etfs.name: VanEck Gold Miners ETF
etfs.desc: Gold miners — a high-beta play on the gold price, only loosely tied to the broad materials cycle.

etfs.name: SPDR S&P Metals & Mining ETF
etfs.desc: Equal-weight metals & mining (steel/aluminum/coal) — the highest-beta industrial-commodity slice.

etfs.name: Real Estate Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Real-Estate sector (US REITs) — cell towers, data centers, industrial, retail. Highly rate-sensitive.

etfs.name: Vanguard Real Estate ETF
etfs.desc: The largest, most liquid US REIT ETF — broader than XLRE (adds specialized/mortgage-adjacent REITs).

etfs.name: Schwab U.S. REIT ETF
etfs.desc: US REITs at the lowest fee in the group — excludes mortgage REITs.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Real Estate ETF
etfs.desc: US real estate incl. some real-estate services; higher fee.

etfs.name: iShares Cohen & Steers REIT ETF
etfs.desc: Concentrated large-cap 'realty majors' — a higher-quality, more concentrated REIT tilt.

etfs.name: Utilities Select Sector SPDR
etfs.desc: The GICS Utilities sector — bond-proxy defensive PLUS the AI-power-demand theme (CEG/VST/NEE). The default utilities vehicle.

etfs.name: Vanguard Utilities ETF
etfs.desc: Broader utilities incl. more mid/small caps at the same low fee.

etfs.name: Fidelity MSCI Utilities ETF
etfs.desc: MSCI US utilities — VPU-like coverage at the lowest fee.

etfs.name: iShares U.S. Utilities ETF
etfs.desc: US utilities, cap-weighted; higher fee.

etfs.name: VanEck Uranium and Nuclear ETF
etfs.desc: Nuclear utilities + uranium miners — the concentrated AI-power / nuclear-renaissance theme, higher fee and beta than XLU.
